title: Best Digital Marketing Software: 30+ Tools (2026) | Salesforce

paragraphs: Digital marketing software includes the platforms that help you manage, automate, and improve how you reach customers online. From email to search to social, these tools support campaigns across multiple channels and help make the most of every interaction.

In 2026, innovation is shifting from manual setup to intelligent execution. This list covers 32 of the most widely used digital marketing software tools today, grouped by function to help you find what fits your needs, whether you're focused on visibility, engagement, or conversion.

Digital marketingsoftware tools are applications designed to support how you promote products and services online. They handle tasks such as email automation, keyword tracking, social media publishing, customer segmentation, and campaignanalytics. You can do all of this from a single platform or connected stack.

These tools help marketers reduce manual work and adapt campaigns based on real-time data. Many platforms also connect with yourCRM, which means your marketing and sales data work together in one view. That alignment makes it easier to track performance, follow up on interest, and measure outcomes more precisely.

There’s no shortage of digital marketing tools out there, but finding the right fit depends on your goals and day-to-day needs. To make things easier, we’ve grouped 32 standout platforms by primary use case—so whether you're refining SEO strategy or looking for thebest email marketing platforms,you’ll find tools designed to support your next move. Each tool featured here has earned a 4-star rating or higher on trusted review sites like G2 and Capterra, meaning you only see top-rated options backed by real user feedback. Explore each category below:

Salesforce Marketing Cloudoffers advanced capabilities for audience segmentation andemailautomation so that you reach the right people with the right message at the right time. Built-in AI tools recommend send times, subject lines, and content variations based on customer behavior.

Mailchimpis known for its approachable design and wide range of built-in tools that are especially helpful fordigital marketing for small businessesand growing teams. It combines email marketing with basic CRM functions so that you can track and manage contacts in one place.

Klaviyois designed for ecommerce businesses that want to create more personalized email and SMS flows. It pulls data directly from storefront platforms to support better targeting, product recommendations, and purchase-based automation.

Constant Contacthelps you build effective email campaigns with customizable templates and built-in tools for contact management. It also supports basic ad creation and integrations with social platforms to extend campaign reach.

Dripfocuses on ecommerce marketing, giving you the ability to tailor automated flows based on customer behavior—from abandoned carts to post-purchase follow-ups. It’s built for brands that want to nurture relationships beyond the first transaction.

Litmusis built to help marketers test, refine, and perfect email campaigns before launch. It’s particularly useful for teams managing multiple platforms, devices, or brand standards, making it easy to catch design issues early.

Salesforce CRMbrings customer data, marketing campaigns, and sales activity into a single view so that you can manage relationships at scale. With automation capabilities in both CRM and Marketing Cloud, you can deliver more timely, relevant interactions across every touchpoint.

HubSpotoffers a CRM that’s especially approachable for small and midsize businesses. Growing teams particularly appreciate how HubSpot ties together marketing automation, email, and sales pipeline tracking without requiring extensive setup.

ZohoCRM focuses on flexibility and affordability. It includes features such as automated task logging and AI-based suggestions to help teams stay on top of deals. With customizable workflows, it’s a strong fit when you want more control without extra cost.

ThisB2B marketing automation toolis designed to support lead nurturing and pipeline acceleration. With deep integration into Salesforce CRM, it helps unify marketing and sales activity while adapting to prospect behavior.

Marketo, an Adobe product, offers high-level marketing automation software with features built for enterprise needs, such as account-based marketing and AI-driven content recommendations. It’s built for marketers who need precision across every campaign stage.

ChatGPTis a conversational AI tool that supports everything from content ideation to data analysis. With features such as code interpretation and document summarization, it’s a helpful partner for marketers juggling multiple formats and touchpoints.

Loomhelps teams share quick video updates without needing formal video production. Whether you’re explaining a campaign plan or providing performance updates, it speeds up communication while adding a personal touch.

Wistiagives marketers tools to host, brand, and analyze video content while capturing leads directly through the player. With A/B testing and integration with CRMs, it turns video into a measurable part of the marketing funnel.

DALL·Eis an image generation tool that creates original visuals based on text prompts. It’s especially helpful for marketers working on tight timelines or campaigns that need fresh, eye-catching visuals without relying on stock libraries.

Demiois built for interactive webinars and automated events. With features such as evergreen sessions and downloadable handouts, it’s a strong choice for marketers who want to educate and engage audiences without running live sessions every time.

Salesforce’sAgentforcefor Marketingfits into this category with its AI-powered personalization and content automation tools. Predictive capabilities help determine when to send, what to say, and how to shape the customer journey so every message lands with more relevance.

Google Analyticsremains a staple for tracking how people find and use your website. It helps surface patterns in user behavior and shows which pages are converting—or losing—visitors. With deeper insights into traffic sources and engagement, marketers can make more informed decisions about where to focus next.

Ahrefsis a go-to tool for SEO professionals focused on content performance and link building. It provides clear visibility into what’s driving traffic, for both your site and your competitors’. That makes it easier to identify keyword gaps, backlink opportunities, and ways to improve your ranking.

Semrushoffers a full suite of tools to support SEO, content marketing, and paid search. It’s built for people who want to centralize research and track performance across campaigns. Whether you’re benchmarking competitors or fine-tuning keyword strategies, it brings everything into one dashboard.

Mozhelps marketers identify technical SEO issues, monitor keyword rankings, and measure domain authority. It’s especially useful when you need clear, actionable guidance to improve visibility in search results without getting buried in data.

Google Search Consolegives direct insight into how your site performs in Google search results. It’s a powerful (and free) resource for monitoring keyword queries, clicks, and indexing errors, which makes it a foundational tool for technical SEO.

WordPresscontinues to be one of the most flexible options for content-driven websites. With a massive library of themes and plugins, it can power anything from personal blogs to full-scale business sites without needing deep development resources.

Salesforce Commerce Cloudsupports both online and in-person selling, making it ideal for brands with complex product catalogs or global audiences. Built with ecommerce at its core, it brings together storefront customization, inventory management, and AI-assisted product recommendations.

Canvamakes design accessible for marketers and content creators who need visuals fast. From social posts to presentations, the drag-and-drop editor paired with AI-powered features helps teams produce polished content without needing a design team.
Unbounceis built for conversion-focused landing pages. With AI-assisted tools and A/B testing baked in, it’s designed to help marketers launch high-performing campaigns without developer bottlenecks.

Hotjarhelps you understand how users actually interact with a site—where they click, scroll, and drop off. Combining heatmaps with user feedback, it gives clear visibility into friction points and areas for UX improvement.

Sprout Socialbrings publishing, engagement, and analytics into one dashboard, so it’s easier to manage brand presence across multiple platforms. It’s especially useful forsocial media marketingand support teams that want to stay connected to customer sentiment and trends.

Bufferfocuses on simplifying the scheduling and publishing process, It’s a go-to favorite for lean teams managing multiple accounts. With built-in performance tracking, it's easy to see what’s working and when your audience is most likely to engage.

Hootsuiteis built for large-scale campaigns and teams juggling multiple social initiatives. Its scheduling, content curation, and monitoring features make it easier to stay organized and on-message across networks.

Later Socialstands out for its visual-first approach, especially on Instagram. The drag-and-drop calendar and hashtag suggestions help marketers plan posts that align with brand aesthetics and drive engagement.

Agorapulsecombines publishing, reporting, and social inbox tools in a way that’s easy to learn and quick to navigate. It’s especially strong for marketers who want to connect social efforts to real outcomes such as leads or conversions.

There’s no universal formula for choosing digital marketing tools—but clarity on your goals, audience, and channels goes a long way. Whether you're focused on campaign automation, deeper analytics, or delivering personalized experiences, the right combination of software can help you work faster and reach further.
Tools like Salesforce Marketing Cloud bring data, content, and automation into one connected platform. Learn more aboutSalesforceMarketing Cloud.

Digital marketing software comes in several main types, each helping with different tasks. One big category isemail marketing software, which helps send and manage email campaigns. Another is social media management tools, for scheduling posts and tracking performance across platforms. You also have analytics software to understand website traffic and customer behavior. Finally, there's advertising software to manage online ads, and content management systems for websites and blogs.

To choose the right digital marketing software, first, think about your specific business needs and goals. Consider your budget and how much you're willing to spend. Look for software that is easy for your team to use and that integrates well with your existing tools. It's also smart to read reviews and try out free trials before making a final decision. Make sure it has the features you need most to grow your business.

Digital marketing software greatly simplifies managing campaigns. It helps you plan and schedule tasks, making sure everything goes out on time. It can automate repetitive actions, like sending follow-up emails, saving your team a lot of effort. Most importantly, it gathers data and provides reports that show you exactly how your campaigns are performing. This information helps you make quick adjustments to improve results and spend yourmarketing budgetwisely.
Generate segments in real time, adapt experiences to every individual, and deliver personalized answers in every channel 24/7.
